question.english: You indicated in the survey that you enjoy listening to music. How has the way you listen to music changed compared to the past? Please describe the differences in detail.
question.korean: 설문조사에서 음악 감상을 좋아한다고 하셨는데요. 과거와 비교해서 음악을 듣는 방식이 어떻게 변했나요? 차이점을 자세히 말씀해 주세요.
question.combo_context: 음악 관련 콤보 질문 중 과거와 현재의 차이점을 대조하여 설명하도록 요구하는 비교 문항입니다.

model_answer.script:
Well, looking back, the way I listen to music has changed drastically over the years.

Back in high school, I used to buy physical formats like CDs and cassette tapes. I distinctly remember having to search high and low at local music shops just to find a specific album. Those CDs would take up space on my shelves, but collecting them was a huge part of my life. Occasionally, I still dust off my old player just to feel nostalgic.

Fast forward to today, everything has completely shifted to digital streaming platforms. I no longer need physical space because I have instant access to millions of tracks anywhere, anytime. Instead of listening to a full album from start to finish, I usually curate a playlist tailored to my daily routine and enjoy it on-the-go. What's even cooler is that personalized recommendations help me discover brand-new music effortlessly.

Having my entire music library in my pocket is just incredible compared to the old days. While I sometimes miss the physical touch of CDs, the convenience of modern streaming is definitely unbeatable.
model_answer.structure_note: [서론] 과거와 현재 음악 감상 방식의 거대한 변화 언급 → [과거 MP & 디테일] 학창 시절 CD 구매, 샅샅이 뒤지던 추억 및 공간 차지 (search high and low, take up space, dust off) → [현재 MP & 디테일] 디지털 스트리밍으로 전환, 즉각적 접근 및 이동 중 맞춤 플레이리스트 감상 (digital streaming platform, instant access, curate a playlist, on-the-go, personalized recommendation) → [감정 및 비교] 과거 추억 대비 현재의 압도적 편리함 감상 → [마무리] 주머니 속 음악 보관함(music library)에 대한 만족감으로 마무리
al_points: 과거 시제(used to buy, remembered having to)와 현재 완결형/현재 시제(has shifted, no longer need)를 명확한 대조 디스코스 마커(Back in..., Fast forward to today...)와 함께 교차하여 시제 조절 능력을 보여줍니다.
단순히 '음악을 스트리밍한다'고 하지 않고 'curate a playlist', 'personalized recommendation', 'instant access' 등 구체적인 세부 상황을 나타내는 고급 동사구/명사구를 활용했습니다.
문장과 문장을 부드럽게 잇는 디스코스 마커('Well, looking back', 'Fast forward to today', 'What's even cooler is that')를 자연스럽게 구사했습니다.

vocab.example: I had to search high and low at local music shops just to find a specific album.
Back in high school, I used to buy physical formats like CDs and cassette tapes.
Those old CD cases used to take up space on my bedroom shelves.

vocab.meaning: ~의 먼지를 털어내다, 오래된 것을 다시 꺼내다
vocab.example: Occasionally, I still dust off my old player just to feel nostalgic.
Everything has completely shifted to digital streaming platforms.
vocab.collocation: subscribe to a digital streaming platform

vocab.example: I have instant access to millions of tracks anywhere, anytime.
I usually curate a playlist tailored to my daily routine.
I can listen to my favorite albums on-the-go using wireless earbuds.
Personalized recommendations help me discover brand-new music effortlessly.
Having my entire music library in my pocket is just incredible.
It took me hours to track down that rare album online.
Unused gadget boxes clutter up my entire desk.
High-speed internet provides seamless access to streaming media.
The app gives tailor-made suggestions based on my listening history.
I backed up all my old tracks into a digital music archive.

vocab_quiz.question: 다음 단어/표현의 올바른 한국어 뜻을 고르세요: 'search high and low'
다음 단어/표현의 올바른 한국어 뜻을 고르세요: 'curate a playlist'
다음 단어/표현의 올바른 한국어 뜻을 고르세요: 'dust off'
문장의 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 단어를 고르세요: 'Streaming apps give us ____ access to millions of songs.'
문장의 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 단어를 고르세요: 'I love listening to energetic tracks when I am ____.'
밑줄 친 부분과 뜻이 가장 가까운 표현을 고르세요: 'I had to <u>search high and low</u> at music shops to find that CD.'
밑줄 친 부분과 뜻이 가장 가까운 표현을 고르세요: 'Algorithms offer <u>personalized recommendations</u> based on user tastes.'

patterns.pattern: Back in [time period], I used to [verb], but fast forward to today, [clause].
patterns.usage_today: Back in high school, I used to buy physical formats, but fast forward to today, everything has completely shifted to digital streaming platforms.
patterns.extra_example: Back in college, I used to work out at the outdoor park, but fast forward to today, I prefer hitting a local indoor gym.

patterns.usage_today: What's even cooler is that personalized recommendations help me discover brand-new music effortlessly.
patterns.extra_example: What's even cooler is that the app membership offers unlimited access to audiobooks as well.

review_words.new_example: I put on my headphones and turn on my favorite songs to escape the daily grind during my evening commute.
On weekends, I love to take a stroll around the neighborhood while listening to relaxing indie music.
Out of the blue, my favorite band announced a world tour and released a surprise single.
When I start exploring new streaming algorithms, I often lose track of time.

review_patterns.pattern: I was desperate to [verb], so I decided to...
review_patterns.new_example: I was desperate to clear my head after a hectic week, so I decided to take a short trip to the beach.
review_patterns.mini_mission: 이 패턴과 오늘 배운 단어 'curate a playlist'를 함께 써서 한 문장 말해보세요: 'I was desperate to boost my energy during workouts, so I decided to curate a playlist of fast-tempo songs.'

checklist: 오늘 배운 핵심 단어(core/extend)를 4개 이상 활용했는가?
과거 시제(used to, had to)와 현재 시제의 대비가 자연스러운가?
Main Point 이후 구체적인 과거/현재의 차이점 디테일을 확장했는가?
옛 추억에 대한 그리움이나 현재의 편리함 등 감정/생각을 언급했는가?
결론부에서 전체 답변을 다잡는 마무리 문장을 말했는가?
어색한 정적 없이 자연스러운 디스코스 마커를 사용했는가?
topic: 과거 vs 현재 비교 문장 만들기
